text: Opening up the abdomen.
Staying as much in the midline as possible.
Going from xiphysternum all the way down to pubic symphysis and down to the sheath there.
Just above the umbilicus.
superior to the umbilicus, you're going to go in here and you'll see that there's some fat in the preperitoneal plane.
That fat there is what you're looking for.
You're going to take your finger and just punch it right through and that will bring you straight into the abdomen.
And that's the quickest way to get into an abdomen.
Alright, so we're right inside the abdomen.
Then to do preperitoneal pelvic packing, we want to get into the extraperitoneal space.
So we don't want to put the packs here.
We want to put the packs inside the extraperitoneal space.
I like to go on both sides of the bladder, bladder in the center.
There's the muscle.
Here's the peritoneum.
So you go into the space between the two and you work like that.
Finger dissection, blunt dissection, all the way down to the presacral space.
And you'll feel the presacral space and usually there'll be lots of blood there and you'll see the blood coming up.
What you want to do is have your swabs opened up like this and you put your swabs in one by one into the preperitoneal space.
It's not a big space, so you'll get one or two swabs in on each side.
And then you do the same on the other side.
Again, on the opposite side of the bladder now, doing a blunt dissection and all the way into the preperitoneal space.
And again, I'm going to do the same and put in one or two swabs.
As many swabs as will go into that space there.
You can put another one on each side and then...
That should tampen out the bleeding.
When you do that, you just need to be careful.
It usually goes hand in hand with pelvic fractures, so you must make sure that you don't cut your fingers on the bone, sharp pieces of bone.
Right, so that is preperitoneal pelvic packing, quite easy.
Just develop the space on each side of the bladder.
Those packs can then be removed 48 hours later, more or less, and you shouldn't have any further bleeding.
